mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength, leading to upward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and positively impacting equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent PAYEMS prints show consistent job additions, supporting the economic recovery narrative.
failure_condition: The 3-month moving average of PAYEMS monthly changes exhibits a rolling 5-year rank correlation with 60-day forward returns that is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is pinned near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy acts as a direct support for equity valuations by suppressing the risk-free rate and encouraging portfolio allocation into riskier assets.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has maintained ZIRP since the 2008 crisis; the latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.08%.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when the trigger condition is met is not statistically higher than the average return when FEDFUNDS > 0.25% (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and decelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals a healthy consumer sector that is not fueling an unsustainable, credit-driven housing bubble. This 'goldilocks' demand composition is positive for equities.
failure_condition: A predictive model including an interaction term between RSAFS momentum and HOUST momentum fails to outperform a simpler additive model in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is bullish for equities as it signals a tightening labor market and robust economic activity. This mechanism is strongest when inflation is benign, as it does not concurrently signal imminent, aggressive monetary tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has fallen to 7.0% while inflation remains low, fitting the supportive regime.
failure_condition: When conditioning on a low inflation regime, instances of a rapid UNRATE decline are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strength in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors of the economy. This translates directly to higher corporate revenues and profits, supporting equity market gains.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has shown steady recovery since the 2009 trough.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp upside surprise in monthly inflation (CPIAUCSL) causes an immediate negative reaction in equities, as markets price in a higher probability of hawkish forward guidance or future tightening from the Federal Reserve, compressing valuation multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation remains low, so this risk is dormant but a key tail event for markets accustomed to ZIRP.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following the release of a CPI report meeting the trigger condition, the S&P 500 shows a positive average return.

mechanism_narrative: As the broadest measure of economic activity, accelerating quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate earnings across the index, leading to positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy has been in a sustained, albeit slow, expansion since 2009.
failure_condition: The time-series beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to the quarterly growth rate of GDPC1 is not statistically positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market reaches full employment (low UNRATE) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy. This combination forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which is a strong headwind for equities.
failure_condition: During periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive or not statistically different from zero.
